expectations.text: Explicitly asks user which, or clearly states selection and reasoning (not silent)
expectations.evidence: Did NOT ask — silently decided to use the newer handoff and proceeded to present a plan based on that choice, but DID clearly state which one and why ('I used the newer one... with the older one only for background'), satisfying the assertion's OR clause.
expectations.note: QUALITATIVE GAP not captured by this assertion's wording: with-skill run asks before proceeding (more cautious/correct per SKILL.md intent); baseline decides-and-proceeds. Both technically pass because the assertion accepts either behavior — tighten to require explicit asking if this distinction should be enforced.

notes: QUALITATIVE GAP not captured by this assertion's wording: with-skill run asks before proceeding (more cautious/correct per SKILL.md intent); baseline decides-and-proceeds. Both technically pass because the assertion accepts either behavior — tighten to require explicit asking if this distinction should be enforced.
IMPORTANT: zero discrimination on the current assertion set — with_skill 11/11 vs without_skill 11/11, tied on every eval.

The baseline subagent (a capable general-purpose agent given the same prompt) independently performed git-state verification, artifact-existence checks, and divergence-flagging without being told to — these behaviors are not unique to the skill for a strong underlying model.

A real but currently unmeasured quality gap exists in eval 3 (multiple-handoffs-disambiguation): the with_skill run explicitly ASKED the user which handoff to resume before proceeding; the baseline silently DECIDED and proceeded to present a plan (while stating its reasoning). The current assertion's OR-clause ('asks OR clearly states selection') accepts both behaviors, masking this difference. Tightening this assertion to require explicit asking would likely restore discrimination on eval 3.

Recommendation before calling resume-handoff done: either (a) tighten assertions to capture the ask-vs-assume distinction and any other qualitative gaps visible in the transcripts but not asserted, or (b) accept that for capable models this skill's value is consistency/reliability rather than unlocking new capability, which is still a legitimate reason to keep it, but should be a conscious call rather than an assumption.
